key_metrics: YTD Operating Cash Flow: -$1.39 billion (down from +$4.11 billion in prior year)
qoq_changes: Revenue increased 12.6% sequentially to $12.54 billion from an implied $11.13 billion in Q1 2025
Net income increased 14.4% sequentially to $3.81 billion from an implied $3.33 billion in Q1 2025
Other operating charges decreased significantly, driving sequential operating margin expansion
new_risks: Severe deterioration in YTD operating cash flow (-$1.39 billion) driven by a massive $9.23 billion negative change in operating assets and liabilities
Heightened uncertainty and potential for significant incremental tax and interest liabilities related to the ongoing IRS tax dispute
management_tone: Bullish on operational efficiency and bottom-line growth, but cautious regarding the ongoing IRS tax dispute and working capital impacts.
analyst_note: Coca-Cola demonstrated excellent operating leverage this quarter, driving a 58% YoY increase in net income despite muted 1.4% top-line growth, largely due to a significant drop in other operating charges. However, the massive $9.2B drag in operating assets and liabilities pushing YTD operating cash flow into negative territory is a major red flag requiring immediate management clarification.
